name: Basic Buttermilk Biscuits
recipeIngredient: Spray shortening and corn-meal for coating pan
recipeInstructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. In a food processor or large bowl, mix 3 cups of the flour with salt, sugar, baking powder, and baking soda. Cut the butter into the dry ingredients until the mixture is crumbly like cornmeal. Mix separately the eggs with the buttermilk, then stir all but 2 tablespoons of this liquid into the dry mixture. Stir just enough to make a uniformly moistened dough. Dust a clean work surface with half the remaining flour and turn the dough out onto the floured surface. Sprinkle the top with the rest of the flour and use floured hands to gently press the dough into a circle about 1/2 inch thick. Cut with a 2-inch or 3-inch biscuit cutter, and place biscuits close together on a greased and corn-meal coated cookie sheet. You will get about 12 biscuits. Brush the tops of the biscuits with the reserved buttermilk egg mixture. Bake in the preheated oven until puffed and brown, about 15 minutes. Cool for a few minutes before serving.
